candidates.comparison: async TP with fused gemm comms compile pass vs standard TP (unfused baseline)
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: yes — compare_two_settings performs exact dict equality on generated text, token IDs, finish reasons, and usage stats across two server configurations
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: yes — the async TP fused gemm comms compile pass changes kernel selection and communication/computation overlap; different GEMM kernels and accumulation order can produce different floating-point results that change greedy-decoded text
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: yes — Not Strong #5: async TP / fused distributed compile-pass parity against unfused baseline; also Not Strong #2: non-compiled vs compiled mode parity hidden inside compare_two_settings (when eager_mode=False, async_tp_args uses VLLM_COMPILE while tp_args uses eager)
candidates.code_snippet:
compare_two_settings(
    model_id,
    async_tp_args,
    tp_args,
    method="generate",
    force_v1_runner=True,
)

candidates.comparison: async TP with SP + fused gemm comms compile pass vs standard TP (NVFP4 model)
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: yes — compare_two_settings performs exact dict equality on generated text, token IDs, finish reasons, and usage stats across two server configurations
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: yes — the compile pass enables SP and fused gemm comms which changes kernel selection, communication patterns, and accumulation order; FP rounding differences can change greedy-decoded text
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: yes — Not Strong #3: different compile strategies / fused distributed compile passes versus baseline; Not Strong #5: async TP / fused distributed compile-pass parity against unfused baseline
candidates.code_snippet:
compare_two_settings(
    NVFP4_MODEL_ID,
    async_tp_args,
    tp_args,
    method="generate",
    force_v1_runner=True,
)

candidates.comparison: sequence parallel compile pass (with various PP/TP/eager/chunked-prefill combos) vs standard TP
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: yes — compare_two_settings performs exact dict equality on generated text, token IDs, finish reasons, and usage stats across two server configurations
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: yes — sequence parallel partitions computation differently across tensor-parallel ranks, changing GEMM shapes and accumulation order; the compile pass itself changes kernel selection; both can produce different FP rounding that changes greedy-decoded text
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: yes — Not Strong #5: sequence parallel compile-pass parity against unfused TP baseline; Not Strong #3: different compile strategies versus baseline (tp_sp_args uses VLLM_COMPILE while tp_args uses default/eager)
candidates.code_snippet:
compare_two_settings(
    model_id,
    tp_sp_args,
    tp_args,
    method="generate",
    force_v1_runner=True,
)

candidates.comparison: sequence parallel compile pass with prompt_embeds enabled vs standard TP
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: yes — compare_two_settings performs exact dict equality on generated text, token IDs, finish reasons, and usage stats across two server configurations
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: yes — sequence parallel changes computation partitioning and kernel selection; prompt_embeds takes a different input path; both can produce different FP rounding that changes greedy-decoded text
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: yes — Not Strong #5: sequence parallel compile-pass parity against unfused TP baseline; Not Strong #3: different compile strategies versus baseline; also Not Strong #9: prompt_embeds path vs standard prompt path when only oracle is final generated text

candidates.comparison: sequence parallel compile pass with NVFP4 quantization (fuse_norm_quant + fuse_act_quant) vs standard TP
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: yes — compare_two_settings performs exact dict equality on generated text, token IDs, finish reasons, and usage stats across two server configurations
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: yes — sequence parallel with fused norm/act quantization changes kernel selection and computation partitioning; NVFP4 quantization kernels are particularly sensitive to FP accumulation order changes
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: yes — Not Strong #5: sequence parallel compile-pass parity against unfused TP baseline; Not Strong #3: different compile strategies / fused distributed compile passes versus baseline
candidates.code_snippet:
_compare_sp(
    NVFP4_MODEL_ID,
    ParallelSetup(tp_size=2, pp_size=1, fuse_norm_quant=True, fuse_act_quant=True, eager_mode=True, chunked_prefill=False),
    "mp", "auto",
    ...
    method="generate",
    is_multimodal=False,
    dtype="bfloat16",
)
